features.name: Complete stepped cylindrical gear shaft body
features.description: Overall one-piece cylindrical shaft with helical external gear section, left Ø7 h6 datum journal, right Ø9 x6 shaft section, right Ø6 k6 datum journal, centre holes at both ends, reliefs/chamfers and gear tooth edge details.

geometry.position_description: Overall axial envelope from left end face to right end face is 61.4 mm; maximum outside diameter taken from gear outside diameter Ø11.79.

features.name: Helical external gear tooth zone
features.description: External right-rising helical gear section, 9 teeth, normal module 1 mm, pressure angle 20°, helix angle 15°, DIN 3972/II basic rack profile, quality DIN 58405 / 8d. The drawing gives outside diameter Ø11.79 with 0/-0.05 tolerance and gear data table values for pitch, base and root diameters. Axial span is shown with dimensions 23 ±0.1, 23.5 ±0.15 and 46.5 ±0.1; the lower view also shows a 20.9 mm toothed visible/profile length. Exact interpretation of the axial gear tooth active length is slightly ambiguous due to the broken view.

geometry.position_description: Helical gear section begins after the left Ø7 h6 journal/relief shoulder and extends through the central broken portion toward the Ø9 x6 right shaft section; axial dimension chain shown as 23 ±0.1 plus 23.5 ±0.15 within a 46.5 ±0.1 span.

surface_finish.notes: Rz 6 surface texture symbol appears at the helical tooth/gear region; Rz 2.5 callouts apply to adjacent journals.

features.material_condition: case-hardened and tempered; gear data quality DIN 58405 / 8d

feature_graph.graph_summary: The part is a small case-hardened 16MnCrS5 stepped cylindrical gear shaft, overall length 61.4 mm, with the shaft axis running left-to-right. The left datum journal A is Ø7 h6 by 9 ±0.1 mm with a DIN 332-R2×4.25 centre hole and shoulder relief/chamfers. The central region is a right-rising 9-tooth external helical gear, normal module 1 mm, helix angle 15°, pressure angle 20°, outside diameter Ø11.79 0/-0.05 and gear-quality requirements from DIN 58405 / 8d; gear-table data include pitch diameter 9.318, base diameter 8.719, generated root diameter 7.727 -0.05, effective root diameter 8.79 -0.05 and gauge over two teeth 4.878 -0.019. To the right is an Ø10 gear-end/step region, a smooth Ø9 x6 shaft section 16 mm long with Rz 2.5 and runout 0.02 to datum axis A-B, then a relief/transition and the right datum journal B Ø6 k6 by 5.9 mm with a DIN 332-R1.6×3 centre hole. Enlarged details A and B define local gear tooth end edge radii/chamfers with R0.4, 15°, 0.2 and 1.45 dimensions. Datums A and B form the common datum axis for runout controls.

clarifications.question: Confirm the exact axial extent of the active helical tooth face: should the gear feature length be treated as the 46.5 ±0.1 mm span, the 23/23.5 mm segmented chain, or the 20.9 mm dimension shown in the lower view?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The main view is broken and the lower view shows a separate 20.9 mm dimension; this affects the reconstructed 3D tooth face width and any downstream volume or inspection interpretation.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use 46.5 ±0.1 mm as the overall geared/shaft span with 20.9 mm recorded as visible/profile tooth length until clarified.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether the Ø10 and Ø11 callouts are separate turned cylindrical steps or gear/root-related reference diameters within the toothed zone.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The drawing shows Ø10 and Ø11 in the gear/transition region, but their exact physical boundaries are partly obscured by tooth graphics and the broken view.
clarifications.suggested_default: Model Ø10 as a coaxial intermediate gear-end shaft step and keep Ø11/Ø11.79 as gear-related diameters.

clarifications.question: Confirm the applicability of the Rz 6 and Rz 2.5 surface texture symbols to the gear flanks, Ø9 x6 shaft, and Ø6 k6 journal surfaces.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The surface texture symbols are near multiple adjacent cylindrical surfaces; exact leader applicability affects feature-level finish requirements.
clarifications.suggested_default: Apply Rz 6 to the gear tooth zone and Rz 2.5 to the datum/bearing journal surfaces as extracted.

clarifications.question: Confirm the intended interpretation of DIN 332-R2×4.25 and DIN 332-R1.6×3 centre-hole dimensions, specifically whether the second value is the required axial depth/length for the centre hole.
clarifications.why_it_matters: DIN 332 centre-hole notation is standard-dependent; depth values were extracted from the callout but may represent standard centre-hole size parameters rather than a directly toleranced depth.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat R2×4.25 and R1.6×3 as centre-hole standard designations with depth/length values 4.25 mm and 3.0 mm respectively.

clarifications.question: What is the depth of F005 (Left annular relief groove at gear shoulder)? No depth dimension was found on the drawing for this feature.
What is the depth of F009 (Right shoulder relief / transition before Ø6 k6 journal)? No depth dimension was found on the drawing for this feature.

guardrail_results.message: Title block and notes were read first; drawing number, material, heat treatment, general tolerance and notes extracted.

Part envelope length uses the visible overall reference dimension (61.4) and maximum diameter uses the largest visible outside diameter Ø11.79 from the gear callout. No ordinate dimensioning was present.
Full scan found no M2/M2.5/M3/M4/M5/M6/M8/M10/M12 or other thread callouts; no thread features created.
Only end centre-hole callouts DIN 332-R2×4.25 and DIN 332-R1.6×3 were found; both are represented as feature nodes.
Centre-hole depths were interpreted from DIN 332 standard callout values; no explicit depth symbol is present. Clarification added for interpretation.
GD&T runout controls reference datums A and B, both defined in the datum scheme.
Every feature has at least one relationship; symmetric coaxial relationships were created where appropriate.
Several gear axial-boundary interpretations are uncertain due to the broken view; best-guess values were extracted with clarifications rather than inventing missing boundaries.

analysis_trace.summary: Identified a single DIN A3 drawing sheet with one main longitudinal shaft view, lower auxiliary/sectional view, enlarged details A and B, an axial end view, gear data table, notes and title block.
issues: Some local boundaries in the toothed zone are obscured by the broken view and helical tooth graphics.

analysis_trace.summary: Main side view supplies most shaft/gear dimensions, datum symbols and GD&T. Lower view confirms centre-hole sections and gear layout. Detail A and B show enlarged tooth-end geometry. The circular end view confirms the external gear tooth pattern.
issues: Detail labels A and B coexist with datum symbols A and B; these were separated carefully.

analysis_trace.summary: Extracted the overall stepped shaft body, datum journals, DIN 332 centre holes, shoulder chamfers/reliefs, helical gear feature with table data, Ø10 intermediate step, Ø9 x6 shaft, right relief, Ø6 k6 datum journal, and detail A/B tooth-edge radii.
issues: Feature type external_cylinder was used for the helical gear because the schema has no dedicated gear feature type.

analysis_trace.summary: Correlated circles/sections/end holes between main and lower views so centre holes are not duplicated; correlated gear table and tooth visuals into one gear feature with separate detail-radius features.
issues: The 20.9 mm lower-view dimension may represent active tooth length rather than full gear span; clarification added.

analysis_trace.summary: Assembled all coaxial cylindrical and centre features around the common X-axis, with reliefs and chamfers linked as machining-dependent edge/transition features.

Embedded diameter fit classes h6, x6 and k6 on their journals; one-sided gear diameter tolerances; axial tolerances 9 ±0.1, 23 ±0.1, 23.5 ±0.15 and 46.5 ±0.1; gear quality/table values; and two runout GD&T controls to datum axis A-B.
issues: Runout symbol interpreted from ISO/German arrow-like control frame; confidence moderate-high.

analysis_trace.summary: Datum A identified on the left Ø7 h6 journal and datum B on the right Ø6 k6 journal. GD&T A-B interpreted as the common datum axis from the two journal axes.
issues: Datum symbols are close to other detail labels; classification is based on boxed datum feature symbols attached to the journal diameter areas.

analysis_trace.summary: Extracted material 16MnCrS5 1.7139, case-hardening/tempering note with 700+100 HV10 and Eht=0.3+0.2 mm, general tolerance DIN ISO 2768-mK, sharp-edge removal/deburr note, RoHS/REACH note, and general surface texture notation Rz16 with Rz6/Rz2.5 exceptions.

Ran thread scan: no threads found. Hole scan: both DIN 332 centre holes captured. Depth scan: no slots/pockets; centre-hole depths interpreted from standard callouts and flagged. Dimension re-read performed for low-confidence Ø10/axial gear-span interpretations. Relationship completeness checked: no orphan features and datum references match datum scheme.
issues: Clarifications retained for gear active length, Ø10/Ø11 interpretation, surface finish applicability, and centre-hole standard-depth interpretation.
